title: Dual-Use Risk - Cyber, Bio, Chem, Nuclear Uplift

questions.question: How did the 2024-2025 bio-uplift narrative evolve?
options: It stayed unchanged across 2024-2025
From 'no uplift' to 'mild uplift'
From 'severe uplift' to 'no measurable uplift'
From 'mild uplift' (2024) to 'on the cusp of meaningfully helping novices create known biological threats' (April 2025) to '2.53x uplift insufficient to rule out ASL-3' (Anthropic 2025)
questions.question_zh: 2024-2025 年间生物风险 uplift 的叙事是如何演变的?
options_zh: 2024-2025 年间没有变化
从「无 uplift」演变为「轻微 uplift」
从「严重 uplift」演变为「无可测量的 uplift」
从 2024 年的「轻微 uplift」演变为 2025 年 4 月的「在协助新手制造已知生物威胁方面已接近实质性帮助的边缘」,再到 Anthropic 2025 年的「2.53 倍 uplift,不足以排除 ASL-3」

questions.question: What did Anthropic's November 2025 cyber threat report find?
options: Chinese-linked state actors used Claude's agentic coding tool to automate 80-90% of a cyberattack campaign, with human intervention only in 4-6 steps
Frontier models refused all cyber tasks
Cyberattacks remained entirely manual
Open-source models exceeded frontier capability
questions.question_zh: Anthropic 2025 年 11 月的网络威胁报告发现了什么?
options_zh: 中国相关国家行为者使用 Claude 的 agentic coding 工具自动化了一次网络攻击行动 80-90% 的环节,人类介入仅出现在 4-6 个步骤
前沿模型拒绝了所有网络任务
网络攻击仍然完全是手工的
开源模型超越了前沿能力

questions.question: How are vision-enabled frontier models eroding the chem/bio execution gap?
options: By replacing all lab equipment with virtual simulators
By generating fake protocols

By observing wet-lab video and providing real-time protocol correction; OpenAI's December 2025 demonstration showed GPT-5 iterating on wet-lab experiments at 79x efficiency improvement via AI-driven protocol optimization
By increasing context length only
questions.question_zh: 支持视觉的前沿模型如何在侵蚀化学 / 生物领域的执行落差?
options_zh: 用虚拟仿真器替换所有实验室设备
通过生成假协议
通过观察 wet-lab 视频并提供实时的协议纠错; OpenAI 在 2025 年 12 月演示了 GPT-5 通过 AI 驱动的协议优化在 wet-lab 实验上获得 79 倍效率提升
仅靠加大上下文长度

questions.question: Why has nuclear-domain uplift remained bounded relative to bio/chem/cyber?
options: Nuclear is the easiest domain to attack with AI

The threat model is dominated by fissile-material acquisition (a non-informational, procurement-and-equipment bottleneck), so AI uplift on the information layer provides limited novice uplift in practice
All nuclear information has been removed from training data
Frontier models refuse to discuss physics
questions.question_zh: 为什么核领域的 uplift 相对于生 / 化 / 网络仍然有限?
options_zh: 核是最容易被 AI 攻击的领域
核威胁模型主要受裂变材料获取支配 (这是一个非信息性的、采购和设备层面的瓶颈),因此 AI 在信息层面的 uplift 在实践中给新手带来的提升有限
训练数据中所有核相关信息都已被移除
前沿模型拒绝讨论物理

questions.question: What does the novice-relative vs expert-absolute asymmetry imply for safety cases?
options: Both are perfectly addressed by RLHF

Addressing only novice uplift (input filters, refusals, uncertainty) is insufficient for expert-absolute control; additional measures like elicitation-hardening, capability unlearning, and control protocols are needed
Only novice uplift matters
Expert-absolute capability is automatically zero
questions.question_zh: 「相对新手」与「绝对专家」的不对称对 safety case 意味着什么?
options_zh: 两者都被 RLHF 完美解决
仅处理新手 uplift (输入过滤、拒答、不确定性) 不足以应对绝对专家级控制; 还需要 elicitation-hardening、能力 unlearning 和 control 协议等额外手段
只有新手 uplift 重要
绝对专家级能力自动为零

questions.question: What is OpenAI's 'trusted access' pilot in the cyber dual-use context?
options: A patch for CVE-2025-32711
An open-source release of GPT-5
Closing all API access
Granting vetted security organisations (incident-response firms, government) capability access for defensive dual-use work, creating an access asymmetry that favours defenders if it scales
questions.question_zh: 在网络两用上下文中,OpenAI 的「trusted access」试点是什么?
options_zh: 针对 CVE-2025-32711 的补丁
GPT-5 的开源版本
关闭所有 API 访问
向经过审核的安全机构 (事件响应公司、政府) 授予防御性两用工作所需的能力访问权限,如果规模化将造就有利于防御者的访问不对称
